MINI MINUTES:

RULING - A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - Defendant Michael Dumas' Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment (Transferred From Dept. 302) IS GRANTED PER CCP 473(b). ATTORNEY KERLESHI IS ORDERED TO PAY PLAINTIFF $9,261.60 AS REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES/COSTS. DEFENDANT MUST COMPLY WITH ALL DISCOVERY ORDERS PREVIOUSLY ENTERED WITHIN 15 DAYS. MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Judge Gerald Ragan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Helen Sorep-Matley.
Dirk van Ausdall, Esq. for Plaintiff: Robin Chiang & Company.
Plaintiff's Witness: David Fung, was sworn and examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant: Michael Dumas as follows:
Damages $20,876.47
Prejudgment Interest $7,489.21
Costs $365.10
Judgment signed in open court.

Mtn For Evidence, Terminating, & For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft Michael Dumas
Off Calendar Sep-13-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions is granted. No opposition has been filed. The Court finds that defendant disobeyed the July 1, 2005 order and that further orders would not result in compliance. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $486.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
